text: The following events were reported: Hail, Lightning, Thunderstorm Wind. These occurred near station USW00003927, approximately 30.8591 km away, between 2014-10-02 14:04:00 and 2014-10-02 17:00:00. The events included records with hailstones measuring 35.20851851851852 inches.The events resulted in 1 injuries and 0 fatalities. The events caused property damage valued at 208000.0 and crop damage of 0.0. Episode Narrative: Thunderstorms developed across north Texas around midday Thursday as a cold front plowed into a warm, moist and unstable air mass in place across the region. Storms began scattered in nature near the Red River and extended south-southwest to just west of the Dallas-Fort Worth Metroplex along the front. Activity intensified as the front pushed southeast into the Metroplex, with storms becoming severe and producing a large swath of wind damage along the I-20 corridor. Large hail along with damaging winds also occurred in northern portions of the Metro area in Denton County. Storms eventually organized into a squall line, which produced additional areas of wind damage farther south across central and southern portion of the region. Thunderstorms developed across north Texas around midday Thursday as a cold front plowed into a warm, moist and unstable air mass in place across the region. Event Narrative: A report of golf-ball sized hail was received via social media in Ponder, TX. A National Weather Service Employee reported that a revolving door was destroyed at the University of North Texas Health Science Center by an estimated 70 MPH wind gust. A social media report of penny-sized hail was received on the campus of Texas Christian University. Emergency management reported one-inch diameter hail on the campus of Texas Christian University. Amateur radio reported one-inch diameter hail near the University of North Texas. Amateur radio reported golf ball sized hail near the intersection of Interstate 35 and Hwy 288 on the north side of Denton. Nickel sized hail and 50 mph winds were reported near the intersection of Loop 820 and Camp Bowie Blvd. Amateur radio reported golf ball sized hail near the intersection of Broadcast Hill and Oakland St. Quarter-sized hail was reported by a storm chaser 2 miles east of Texas Christian University. Amateur radio reported that an unoccupied car was crushed be a large tree near the intersection of Scenery Hill and Oakland Street. Golf ball sized hail was reported by amateur radio at the Texas Motor Speedway. Amateur radio reported one-inch diameter hail on White Settlement Road just west of Loop 820. Amateur radio reported that 3 to 4 inch diameter tree limbs were blown down near the intersection of Bryant Irvin Rd and Camp Bowie Blvd. The Police Department reported an estimated wind gust of 60 MPH on the campus of Texas Christian University. Amateur radio recorded a wind gust of 70 MPH near the intersection of Broadcast Hill and Oakland St. Broadcast media reported that a section of roofing at Arlington High School was lifted off and blown onto Cooper Street. Amateur radio recorded a wind gust of 75 MPH near the intersection of Randol Mill Rd and Fielder Rd. Amateur radio reported that a 2.5 foot diameter tree was uprooted by thunderstorm winds near Randol Mill Park. Amateur radio measured a wind gust of 65 MPH near the intersection of Bowen Rd and Arkansas Ln. Amateur radio reported that trees with 12 inch diameter bases and 6 inch diameter limbs were blown down near the intersection of Randol Mill Rd and Fielder Rd. A public report of trees blown down on Park Row in Arlington was received. A 51 MPH wind gust was reported at Arlington Municipal Airport. A 59 knot wind gust was measured by a member of the general public in Euless, TX. Amateur radio reported that 18 to 20-inch based trees were blown down near the 2000 block of east Handley St. Lightning caused a house fire in the 12200 block of Blue Ridge Drive in Frisco, TX. A chunk of roofing flew off of the girls dorm, and a car was smashed by a falling tree. One of the students received lacerations from broken glass. A report was received via social media of large tree branches being knocked down in Mansfield. A report was received via social media of trees down on roofs.

Activity intensified as the front pushed southeast into the Metroplex, with storms becoming severe and producing a large swath of wind damage along the I-20 corridor. Large hail along with damaging winds also occurred in northern portions of the Metro area in Denton County. Storms eventually organized into a squall line, which produced additional areas of wind damage farther south across central and southern portion of the region. Thunderstorms developed across north Texas around midday Thursday as a cold front plowed into a warm, moist and unstable air mass in place across the region. Event Narrative: A report of golf-ball sized hail was received via social media in Ponder, TX. A National Weather Service Employee reported that a revolving door was destroyed at the University of North Texas Health Science Center by an estimated 70 MPH wind gust. A social media report of penny-sized hail was received on the campus of Texas Christian University. Emergency management reported one-inch diameter hail on the campus of Texas Christian University. Amateur radio reported one-inch diameter hail near the University of North Texas. Amateur radio reported golf ball sized hail near the intersection of Interstate 35 and Hwy 288 on the north side of Denton. Nickel sized hail and 50 mph winds were reported near the intersection of Loop 820 and Camp Bowie Blvd. Amateur radio reported golf ball sized hail near the intersection of Broadcast Hill and Oakland St. Quarter-sized hail was reported by a storm chaser 2 miles east of Texas Christian University. Amateur radio reported that an unoccupied car was crushed be a large tree near the intersection of Scenery Hill and Oakland Street. Golf ball sized hail was reported by amateur radio at the Texas Motor Speedway. Amateur radio reported one-inch diameter hail on White Settlement Road just west of Loop 820. Amateur radio reported that 3 to 4 inch diameter tree limbs were blown down near the intersection of Bryant Irvin Rd and Camp Bowie Blvd. The Police Department reported an estimated wind gust of 60 MPH on the campus of Texas Christian University. Amateur radio recorded a wind gust of 70 MPH near the intersection of Broadcast Hill and Oakland St. Broadcast media reported that a section of roofing at Arlington High School was lifted off and blown onto Cooper Street. Amateur radio recorded a wind gust of 75 MPH near the intersection of Randol Mill Rd and Fielder Rd. Amateur radio reported that a 2.5 foot diameter tree was uprooted by thunderstorm winds near Randol Mill Park. Amateur radio measured a wind gust of 65 MPH near the intersection of Bowen Rd and Arkansas Ln. Amateur radio reported that trees with 12 inch diameter bases and 6 inch diameter limbs were blown down near the intersection of Randol Mill Rd and Fielder Rd. A public report of trees blown down on Park Row in Arlington was received. A 51 MPH wind gust was reported at Arlington Municipal Airport. A 59 knot wind gust was measured by a member of the general public in Euless, TX. Amateur radio reported that 18 to 20-inch based trees were blown down near the 2000 block of east Handley St. Lightning caused a house fire in the 12200 block of Blue Ridge Drive in Frisco, TX. A chunk of roofing flew off of the girls dorm, and a car was smashed by a falling tree. One of the students received lacerations from broken glass. A report was received via social media of large tree branches being knocked down in Mansfield. A report was received via social media of trees down on roofs.

The events caused property damage valued at 208000.0 and crop damage of 0.0. Episode Narrative: Thunderstorms developed across north Texas around midday Thursday as a cold front plowed into a warm, moist and unstable air mass in place across the region. Storms began scattered in nature near the Red River and extended south-southwest to just west of the Dallas-Fort Worth Metroplex along the front. Activity intensified as the front pushed southeast into the Metroplex, with storms becoming severe and producing a large swath of wind damage along the I-20 corridor. Large hail along with damaging winds also occurred in northern portions of the Metro area in Denton County. Storms eventually organized into a squall line, which produced additional areas of wind damage farther south across central and southern portion of the region. Thunderstorms developed across north Texas around midday Thursday as a cold front plowed into a warm, moist and unstable air mass in place across the region. Event Narrative: A report of golf-ball sized hail was received via social media in Ponder, TX. A National Weather Service Employee reported that a revolving door was destroyed at the University of North Texas Health Science Center by an estimated 70 MPH wind gust. A social media report of penny-sized hail was received on the campus of Texas Christian University. Emergency management reported one-inch diameter hail on the campus of Texas Christian University. Amateur radio reported one-inch diameter hail near the University of North Texas. Amateur radio reported golf ball sized hail near the intersection of Interstate 35 and Hwy 288 on the north side of Denton. Nickel sized hail and 50 mph winds were reported near the intersection of Loop 820 and Camp Bowie Blvd. Amateur radio reported golf ball sized hail near the intersection of Broadcast Hill and Oakland St. Quarter-sized hail was reported by a storm chaser 2 miles east of Texas Christian University. Amateur radio reported that an unoccupied car was crushed be a large tree near the intersection of Scenery Hill and Oakland Street. Golf ball sized hail was reported by amateur radio at the Texas Motor Speedway. Amateur radio reported one-inch diameter hail on White Settlement Road just west of Loop 820. Amateur radio reported that 3 to 4 inch diameter tree limbs were blown down near the intersection of Bryant Irvin Rd and Camp Bowie Blvd. The Police Department reported an estimated wind gust of 60 MPH on the campus of Texas Christian University. Amateur radio recorded a wind gust of 70 MPH near the intersection of Broadcast Hill and Oakland St. Broadcast media reported that a section of roofing at Arlington High School was lifted off and blown onto Cooper Street. Amateur radio recorded a wind gust of 75 MPH near the intersection of Randol Mill Rd and Fielder Rd. Amateur radio reported that a 2.5 foot diameter tree was uprooted by thunderstorm winds near Randol Mill Park. Amateur radio measured a wind gust of 65 MPH near the intersection of Bowen Rd and Arkansas Ln. Amateur radio reported that trees with 12 inch diameter bases and 6 inch diameter limbs were blown down near the intersection of Randol Mill Rd and Fielder Rd. A public report of trees blown down on Park Row in Arlington was received. A 51 MPH wind gust was reported at Arlington Municipal Airport. A 59 knot wind gust was measured by a member of the general public in Euless, TX. Amateur radio reported that 18 to 20-inch based trees were blown down near the 2000 block of east Handley St. Lightning caused a house fire in the 12200 block of Blue Ridge Drive in Frisco, TX. A chunk of roofing flew off of the girls dorm, and a car was smashed by a falling tree. One of the students received lacerations from broken glass. A report was received via social media of large tree branches being knocked down in Mansfield. A report was received via social media of trees down on roofs.
